commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `claude-opus-4-8` to the built-in Claude pricing table, updates the generated parser hash, and adds pricing/scanner regression tests for stale or missing models.dev fallback behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main falls back to a built-in Claude pricing table that has no `claude-opus-4-8` key, so a missing or stale models.dev lookup returns nil. I did not execute Swift tests in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the open PR already contains the focused fix and should proceed through normal maintainer and CI review.

target.title: Add Opus 4.8 to built-in Claude pricing table
target.body:
## Summary
Opus 4.8 token cost displayed as empty (#1210). `claudeCostUSD` tries the
models.dev catalog first, then falls back to the built-in Claude pricing table.
That table had entries through Opus 4.7 but none for `claude-opus-4-8`, so when
the models.dev cache is missing or stale the lookup returns nil and the cost
card renders empty.

This adds a `claude-opus-4-8` entry mirroring the existing Opus 4.5/4.6/4.7
entries: $5 input / $25 output / $6.25 cache-write / $0.50 cache-read per 1M
tokens. Values confirmed against the live models.dev catalog, which lists Opus
4.8 identically to 4.7. `normalizeClaudeModel`'s dated-suffix stripping means a
future `claude-opus-4-8-YYYYMMDD` id resolves to this entry too.

## Tests
- `CostUsagePricingTests."claude cost supports opus48"` — built-in table prices Opus 4.8 to the exact expected value. Points at a fresh empty cache root so it proves the built-in fallback rather than passing on a populated models.dev cache.
- `CostUsageScannerClaudeRegressionTests."claude opus 4 8 issue row gets priced"` — end-to-end: a Claude JSONL row with `claude-opus-4-8` now produces a non-zero cost.

## Real behavior proof
Running the `codexbar` CLI against a synthetic Opus 4.8 usage row, with a stale
models.dev cache that predates Opus 4.8 (i.e. the exact bug condition — the
lookup misses and falls through to the built-in table):

```
$ CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=<tmp> codexbar cost --provider claude --refresh
Claude Cost (API-rate estimate)
Today: $0.28 · 72K tokens
```

JSON output (exact):
```json
"modelBreakdowns": [
  { "modelName": "claude-opus-4-8", "cost": 0.28375, "totalTokens": 71700 }
]
```

Before this change the same row rendered an empty cost.

## Verification
`swiftformat Sources Tests --lint` and `swiftlint --strict` both clean.

Fixes #1210
target.author: devyrpauli
